title: ISO 11137-1:2025 -- Sterilization of health care products -- Radiation -- Part 1: Requirements for the development, validation and routine control of a sterilization process for medical devices
authors_or_org: International Organization for Standardization (ISO)
claim_supported: Second edition (2025) of ISO 11137-1 cancels and replaces the first edition (ISO 11137-1:2006), covering radiation sterilization (Co-60/Cs-137 gamma, electron-beam, X-ray) validation and routine control for medical devices. Key 2025 changes: allowable electron-beam energy raised from 10 MeV to 11 MeV and X-ray energy from 5 MeV to 7.5 MeV; ISO 13004 and ASTM 52628 added as normative references; dose-audit frequency shifted from quarterly to every four months (4/year); VDmax dose options expanded in 2.5 kGy increments; dosimetry-testing language clarified to allow parametric/machine-based batch release in well-controlled facilities.
locator: Content confirmed via International Irradiation Association (IIA) news article (iiaglobal.com/iia-news/new-revision-of-iso-11137-12025-published/), fetched directly this session, since the ISO catalogue page itself returns HTTP 403 to direct fetch
notes: ISO's own catalogue/purchase page is bot-blocked (403, confirmed on two attempts); scope and edition content instead independently confirmed via IIA (a recognized industry trade association), which is an acceptable venue-content confirmation source. The standard's identity (ISO 11137-1:2025) itself is not in doubt.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: IEC 60060-1:2025 -- High-voltage test techniques -- Part 1: General terminology and test requirements
authors_or_org: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
claim_supported: Fourth edition (published 17 April 2025, 164 pages, maintained by IEC Technical Committee 42) of IEC 60060-1 cancels and replaces the third edition (2010), defining terminology and test requirements for dielectric tests with direct, alternating, impulse, and combined/composite high voltages for equipment with Um above 1.0 kV AC / 1.5 kV DC. Key updates: extended positive tolerance of lightning-impulse front time for Um > 800 kV; new Annex C (manual calculation from graphical waveforms) and informative Annex F (new front-time definition for switching-impulse voltage).

notes: No new-unit list price found this session (vendor requires direct quote); the scout's previously found secondary-market Exapro resale listing remains the closest price signal but was not re-verified this session.

title: NPCT (New Parametric Current Transformer) beam-current-monitor datasheet and price list
authors_or_org: Bergoz Instrumentation
claim_supported: Bergoz Instrumentation (France) manufactures the NPCT non-destructive DC beam-current-measurement device (full-scale ranges +/-20 mA to +/-20 A, resolution down to 0.5 uA rms, DC-10 kHz bandwidth, <=0.1% linearity error), used for machine tuning, commissioning, operation, and personnel safety across particle accelerators; the product page links a dated 'Price List' (FY26).

notes: Confirms a named, dated price list exists (stronger-than-typical market-industry signal), though exact figures were not extracted this session.

title: Communications & Power Industries (CPI) -- corporate and product overview
authors_or_org: Communications & Power Industries LLC (CPI)
claim_supported: As fetched this session, cpii.com now describes CPI's remaining business as antennas (earth-station, radar, telemetry, VSAT), solid-state and vacuum-tube amplifiers, medical radiographic/fluoroscopic generators, and structural/radome components -- IMPORTANT CORRECTION: the page states the Electron Device Business (EDB, which includes CPI's klystrons/TWTs/gyrotrons/magnetrons -- the vendors directly relevant to this lane) has been sold off and now operates independently at cpi-edb.com, and that the Palo Alto and Woodland operations transitioned to a separate entity, MPP Inc.
locator: Company website fetched directly this session, cpii.com
notes: MISSION-RELEVANT CORRECTION: the scout's claim (CPI as a unified ~$733M-revenue klystron/TWT/gyrotron/SSPA/medical-X-ray maker under Veritas Capital) is now only partly accurate -- the klystron/vacuum-electron-device business has been corporately separated into CPI Electron Device Business (cpi-edb.com), which returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch this session and could not be independently verified. A future wave should attempt to verify cpi-edb.com and MPP Inc. directly, since those (not the current cpii.com entity) are the actual klystron/TWT/magnetron competitors relevant to this lane. Revenue/employee/ownership figures from the scout's RocketReach-sourced claim remain unverified this session and are dropped from claim_supported pending re-confirmation against the correct current entity.

title: The conceptual design of the high-efficiency 400 kW solid-state power station at 352 MHz for the European spallation source
authors_or_org: Authors not itemized via direct Crossref works fetch this session (International Journal of Microwave and Wireless Technologies; European Spallation Source RF group)
